landing.badge: Journal in English · IELTS-grade feedback + personal growth tracking
landing.headline: Write your life in English.
landing.subheadline: Keep a journal in English and InkPath gives you two things at once: an IELTS band score with line-by-line corrections to level up your writing, and a private space to track your moods, themes, and reflections over time. One habit, two kinds of growth.
landing.feature1Desc: Four-layer analysis on every entry: a 0-9 band score, line-by-line corrections, a band-up rewrite, and extracted vocabulary — all calibrated to official IELTS rubrics.
landing.feature2Title: A Mirror for Your Inner Life
landing.feature2Desc: InkPath quietly tracks the moods, themes, and turning points in your entries. Open the Growth dashboard and see patterns you couldn't see from inside the day.
landing.feature3Desc: Every rewrite extracts useful expressions and saves them to your notebook by CEFR level. Spaced repetition makes sure they end up in your active writing.
landing.feature4Desc: Journals demand absolute trust. End-to-end encryption, no training on your data, one-click export and deletion. We built InkPath because we wanted it to exist for ourselves.
landing.step1Desc: Today's small moments, a reflection, an IELTS essay, an email you need to send. Any topic, any length.
landing.step2Desc: On the language side: band score, corrections, a rewrite, new vocabulary. On the inner side: emotional themes and a reflection prompt — in 30 seconds.
landing.step3Desc: Your band score climbs week by week. Your mood map fills in. You start to recognize the patterns of your own life.
landing.privacyDesc: Journaling requires absolute trust. InkPath uses end-to-end encryption — your entries are encrypted before they leave your device. We literally cannot read them, and we never train on them.
landing.privacyBullet2: Zero training on your personal data
landing.privacyBullet4: Open data — your writing belongs to you
landing.maxFeature2: Premium AI (deeper analysis with Claude Opus)

write.title: What's on your mind?
write.placeholder: Start writing... A diary entry, an observation, a reflection, an essay — anything you want to express.

vocabulary.title: Your Vocabulary
vocabulary.noWords: Your vocabulary notebook is empty. Start writing to build it up!
